properties.name: Bandilngan — Windjana Gorge Mini-Tunnel
properties.comment: To enter the famous gorge trail you walk through a small natural split-rock tunnel corridor. Instant cave vibe before a massive canyon filled with freshwater crocodiles.

properties.name: Cosy Corner Sea Caves
properties.comment: South of the car park, visible along the limestone cliffs of the beach.

properties.name: Calgardup Cave
properties.comment: Torches and helmets provided. Great boardwalks over seasonal water features.

properties.name: C-96 Anomaly Cave Exterior
properties.comment: Cape Range has over 700 highly sensitive wild karst caves. Entry not permitted due to safety and protection of rare bats and troglobites.

properties.name: Cave Point
properties.location: Fitzgerald River National Park, Hopetoun / Bremer Bay
properties.comment: Incredible coastal viewing platform looking directly over a collapsed limestone and spongolite sea cave where the Southern Ocean crashes into the cliff base.

properties.name: Crystal Cave
properties.comment: Perth's only metropolitan tourist cave. Main tour requires a ticket. Surrounding paths show collapsed limestone karst depressions and historic stone infrastructure.

properties.name: Dimalurru — Tunnel Creek
properties.comment: WA's oldest cave system. A breathtaking 750m natural tunnel through the reef. Cavernous entry archway visible just a short walk from the car park.

properties.name: Fern Pool & Circular Pool Caverns
properties.comment: Massive collapsed rock amphitheatres with overhanging ironstone ledges, hidden mossy grottos and small rock cavities. Scenic rather than deep subterranean.

properties.name: Giants Cave
properties.comment: Pitch black self-guided exploration. Vertical ladder climbs, boulder scrambles and tight squeezes through the Chimney. Hard hats and torches mandatory.

properties.name: Jewel Cave
properties.comment: WA's largest show cave. Home to one of the longest straw stalactites in the world.

properties.name: Lake Cave
properties.comment: Features a stunning Suspended Table formation hanging over a permanent crystal lake.

properties.name: Mammoth Cave
properties.comment: Highly accessible. Famous for housing ancient megafauna fossils.

properties.name: Mandu Mandu Gorge Rock Shelters
properties.comment: Easy walking trail through a dry ancient riverbed with shallow open-face limestone cave shelters in the sheer canyon walls visible from the path.

properties.name: Mimbi Caves
properties.location: Emanuel Range, east of Fitzroy Crossing
properties.access: 2WD to meeting point — 4WD track to site
properties.comment: Complex maze of deep limestone fissures and marine fossil walls. Inner sanctuaries require a guided Gooniyandi cultural tour. Outer karst portals visible from base tracks.

properties.name: Mulka's Cave
properties.comment: Iconic inland cave near Wave Rock. No deep caving gear needed. Notable for a vast collection of ancient Indigenous handprint art.

properties.name: Mullet Lake Sea Caves
properties.comment: Small limestone fractures and low-ceiling cave entries along coastal tracks east of Esperance. Best viewed from the exterior.

properties.name: Ngilgi Cave
properties.comment: Located at the northern end of the ridge. Rich Indigenous cultural significance.

properties.name: The Blowholes and Sea Tunnels
properties.comment: Easy paved walk along dramatic granite coastline. Look right down into coastal fractures where the ocean forces air and water through the rock chambers.
